pubmed_links: [Xu et al. (2012) De novo gene mutations highlight patterns of genetic and neural complexity in schizophrenia](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/23042115/); [Levy et al. (2015) Deletion of Rapgef6 disrupts amygdala function in mice](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26057047/); [Maeta et al. (2018) Comprehensive behavioral analysis of mice deficient in Rapgef2 and Rapgef6](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29747665/)
summary: RAPGEF6, a guanine nucleotide exchange factor for Rap1 GTPase, was identified as a schizophrenia susceptibility gene through de novo loss-of-function copy number variants (exon 2–11 deletion) in human patients; mouse knockout models show disrupted amygdala-dependent fear conditioning, hyperlocomotion, and working memory deficits consistent with schizophrenia-like endophenotypes. (emerging evidence)
